title: S&P 500 profit eyes sharpest quarterly growth in four years after Big Tech results
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: S&P 500 profits are projected to grow 27.8% in the first quarter, marking the sharpest quarterly growth in four years, driven by strong earnings from Big Tech c
excerpt: S&P 500 profits are projected to grow 27.8% in the first quarter, marking the sharpest quarterly growth in four years, driven by strong earnings from Big Tech companies. This growth surpasses earlier expectations and signals robust corporate performance.

content.introduction: S&P 500 profits are projected to grow 27.8% in the first quarter, marking the sharpest quarterly growth in four years, driven by strong earnings from Big Tech companies. This growth surpasses earlier expectations and signals robust corporate performance.
body: S&P 500 first-quarter earnings growth is now projected at 27.8%, the fastest pace since late 2021, after Apple, Alphabet, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft all reported results that beat consensus estimates. The LSEG IBES data marks a sharp upgrade from the 16.1% growth expected just a week earlier. The tech earnings fueled a rally in semiconductor stocks, including Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, as analysts pointed to a combined $660 billion capex plan from the U.S. giants that signals sustained AI investment. The first-quarter earnings season is shaping up to be the most powerful for Corporate America since the post-pandemic boom, with blockbuster results from the biggest technology companies driving S&P 500 profit growth toward its fastest pace in more than four years. Analysts now expect aggregate earnings for the benchmark index to surge 27.8% in the first quarter compared to a year earlier, according to data from LSEG IBES released Friday.
